reasoning: M1 Not Seasonally Adjusted shows consistent intra-month growth throughout March due to tax refund distributions depositing into consumer checking accounts. The FRED data demonstrates an unbroken upward streak throughout February 2026. March historically maintains this seasonal expansion prior to mid-April tax payments. Thus, M1 on March 8 and March 31 is highly likely to exceed the March 1 baseline. Following instructions, the longer horizon estimate decays closer to base rates to reflect compounded uncertainty over broader economic conditions and potential impacts from Fed quantitative tightening by late March.
